535171.explanation:
{tex}\vec p{/tex} and {tex}\vec q{/tex} are collinear if {tex}\vec{p}=\lambda \vec{q}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} (x - 4){tex}\vec{a}+\bar{b}{/tex} = {tex}\lambda{/tex}[(x + 2){tex}\vec{a}-\vec{b}{/tex}]
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} x - 4 = {tex}\lambda{/tex}(x + 2) ...(i)
and 1 = {tex}-\lambda{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \lambda{/tex} = -1
From (i),
x - 4 = -1(x + 2) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 1

535187.explanation:
|z1| = a, |z2| = b, |z3| = c
{tex}\left|\begin{array}{lll} a & b & c \\ c & a & b \\ b & c & a \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 0 {tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} a3 + b3 + c3 - 3abc = 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} (a + b + c) (a2 + b2 + c2 - ab - bc - ca) = 0
|z1| = a etc. and z1, z2, z3 are non zero
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a + b + c {tex}\ne{/tex} 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a2 + b2 + c2 - ab - bc - ca = 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \frac{1}{2}{/tex} [(a - b)2 + (b - c)2 + (c - a)2] = 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} a = b = c
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} |z1| = |z2| = |z3|
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} The vertices representing z1, z2 and z3 are equidistant from the origin.
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} The circumcentre must be at the origin.

535197.explanation:
I1 = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin ^{2} x}{\sin ^{2} x}{/tex}dx = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} d x=[x]_{0}^{\pi / 2}=\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}
I2 = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin ^{2} 2 x}{\sin ^{2} x}{/tex}dx = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac{n}{2}}{/tex}4cos2 xdx
= 2{tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex}(1 + cos 2x)dx
= {tex}2\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)+\frac{2}{2}[\sin 2 x]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\pi{/tex}
I3 = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin ^{2} 3 x}{\sin ^{2} x}{/tex}dx = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\left(3 \sin x-4 \sin ^{3} x\right)^{2}}{\sin ^{2} x}{/tex}dx
= {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex}(9 - 24sin2x + 16sin4x)dx
= {tex}\frac{9 \pi}{2}-24\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)+16\left(\frac{3 \cdot 1}{4 \cdot 2} \cdot \frac{\pi}{2}\right){/tex}
= {tex}\frac{3 \pi}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2I2 = I1 + I3
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} I1, I2, I3 are in A.P.

535206.explanation:
The line {tex}\frac{x-3}{1}=\frac{y+2}{-1}=\frac{z+\lambda}{-2}{/tex} lies in the plane 2x - 4y + 3z = 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (3, -2, -{tex}\lambda{/tex}) lies in the plane 2x - 4y + 3z = 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2(3) - 4(-2) + 3 (-{tex}\lambda{/tex}) = 2
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 14 - 3{tex}\lambda{/tex} = 2 {tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 4
Shortest distance between the lines
{tex}\frac{x-3}{1}=\frac{y+2}{-1}=\frac{z+4}{-2}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{x-1}{12}=\frac{y}{9}=\frac{z}{4}{/tex} is
d = {tex}\frac{\left|\begin{array}{ccc} x_{2}-x_{1} & y_{2}-y_{1} & z_{2}-z_{1} \\ l_{1} & m_{1} & n_{1} \\ l_{2} & m_{2} & m_{2} \end{array}\right|}{\sqrt{\left(m_{1} n_{2}-m, n_{1}\right)^{2}+\left(l_{2} n_{1}-l_{1} n_{2}\right)^{2}+\left(l_{1} m_{2}-l_{2} m_{1}\right)^{2}}}{/tex}
{tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} x_{2}-x_{1} & y_{2}-y_{1} & z_{2}-z_{1} \\ l_{1} & m_{1} & n_{1} \\ l_{2} & m_{2} & m_{2} \end{array}\right|=\left|\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & 2 & 4 \\ 1 & -1 & -2 \\ 12 & 9 & 4 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Shortest distance (d) = 0

535213.explanation:
f(x) = tan x - tan3x + tan5x - ...
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f(x) = {tex}\frac{\tan x}{1+\tan ^{2} x}{/tex} ...[Infinite G.P.]
= sin x cos x = {tex}\frac{\sin 2 x}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} {/tex}f(x)dx = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{\sin 2 x}{2}{/tex}dx = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{-\cos 2 x}{2}\right]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}{/tex}
= {tex}-\frac{1}{4}{/tex}(0 - 1) = {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex}

535229.explanation:
Given, A, B, C are collinear.
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{\mathrm{AB}}=\vec{\mathrm{BC}} m \cdot \vec{\mathrm{BC}}{/tex}; for any scalar m.
{tex}\Rightarrow-\hat{i}+3 \hat{j}-5 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}=m \cdot((\lambda+3) \hat{i}+(\mu-4) \hat{j}+5 \hat{k}){/tex}
Comparing the coefficients of {tex}\hat{i}, \hat{j}{/tex} and {tex}\hat{k}{/tex}, we get
5m = -5 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} m = -1
and m({tex}\lambda{/tex} + 3) = -1 {tex}\Rightarrow \lambda{/tex} = -2
and m({tex}\mu{/tex} - 4) = 3 {tex}\Rightarrow \mu{/tex} = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{c}=-2 \hat{i}+\hat{j}+3 \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}|\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}|{/tex} = {tex}\mid(-2 \hat{i}+\hat{j}+3 \hat{k})+(-3 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}){/tex}{tex}+(-2 \hat{i}+\hat{j}+3 \hat{k}) \mid{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow|\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}|=|-7 \hat{i}+6 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k}|{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{49+36+16}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{101}{/tex}

535253.explanation:
In I2, let x = {tex}\frac{1}{t}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = {tex}-\frac{1}{t^{2}}{/tex}dt
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I2 = {tex}-\int \limits_{1}^{\sin \theta} \frac{t}{1+t^{2}}{/tex}dt {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I2 = -I1
{tex}\Rightarrow \left|\begin{array}{ccc} \mathrm{I}_{1} & \mathrm{I}_{1}^{2} & \mathrm{I}_{2} \\ e^{\mathrm{I}_{1}+\mathrm{I}_{2}} & \mathrm{I}_{2}{ }^{2} & -1 \\ 1 & \mathrm{I}_{1}^{2}+\mathrm{I}_{2}^{2} & -1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} \mathrm{I}_{1} & \mathrm{I}_{1}^{2} & -\mathrm{I}_{1} \\ \mathrm{e}^{0} & \mathrm{I}_{1}{ }^{2} & -1 \\ 1 & 2 \mathrm{I}_{1}{ }^{2} & -1 \end{array}\right|{/tex}
= {tex}\left|\begin{array}{lll} \mathrm{I}_{1} & \mathrm{I}_{1}{ }^{2} & -\mathrm{I}_{1} \\ 1 & \mathrm{I}_{1}^{2} & -1 \\ 0 & \mathrm{I}_{1}^{2} & 0 \end{array}\right|{/tex} ...[By R3 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} R3 - R2]
= I1(0 + {tex}\mathrm{I}_{1}^{2}{/tex}) - I1({tex}\mathrm{I}_{1}^{2}{/tex} - 0) = 0

535269.explanation:
{tex}\vec{a}+2 \vec{b}{/tex} is collinear with {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}+3 \vec{c}{/tex} is collinear with {tex}\vec{a}{/tex}.
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a}+2 \vec{b}=x \vec{c}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}+3 \vec{c}=y \vec{a}{/tex} for some x, y {tex}\in{/tex} R
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a}+2 \vec{b}+6 \vec{c}{/tex} = (x + 6) {tex}\vec{c}{/tex}
Also, {tex}\vec{a}+2 \vec{b}+6 \vec{c}{/tex} = (1 + 2y) {tex}\vec{a}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x + 6){tex}\vec{c}{/tex} = (1 + 2y){tex}\vec{a}{/tex}
{tex}\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} are non-collinear.
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + 6 = 0 and 1 + 2y = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = -6 and {tex}y=-\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a}+2 \vec{b}=x \vec{c}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a}+2 \vec{b}+6 \vec{c}=\vec{0}{/tex}

535292.explanation:
Given, direction ratios are proportional to 2, - 1, 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Direction cosines = {tex}(2 \lambda,-\lambda, 2 \lambda){/tex}
l2 + m2 + n2 = 1
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} {tex}(2 \lambda)^{2}+(-\lambda)^{2}+(2 \lambda)^{2}{/tex} = 1
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} {tex}9 \lambda^{2}{/tex} = 1 {tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} {tex}\lambda{/tex} = {tex}\pm \frac{1}{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Direction cosines are {tex}\left(\pm \frac{2}{3}, \mp \frac{1}{3}, \pm \frac{2}{3}\right){/tex}
But it makes obtuse angle with x-axis.
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} l = {tex}-\frac{2}{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} The component of {tex}\vec r{/tex} along x-axis
= {tex}6 \times\left(-\frac{2}{3}\right){/tex} = -4

535300.explanation:
The given system of equations has a non-zero solution, if {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} \lambda & 1 & 1 \\ -1 & \lambda & 1 \\ -1 & -1 & \lambda \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \lambda{/tex} ({tex}\lambda^{2}{/tex} + 1) -1 (-{tex}\lambda{/tex} + 1) + 1(1 + {tex}\lambda{/tex}) = 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \lambda^{3}{/tex} + 3{tex}\lambda{/tex} = 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \lambda{/tex}({tex}\lambda^{2}{/tex} + 3) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow \lambda{/tex} = 0

535308.explanation:
Any vector coplanar with vectors {tex}\hat{i}+2 \vec{j}-\hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex} is
{tex}\vec{r}=x(\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-\hat{k})+y(\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{r}=x \hat{i}+(2 x+y) \hat{j}+(2 y-x) \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\hat{r}{/tex} is parallel to {tex}\hat{i}+\hat{j}-3 \hat{k}{/tex}.
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{r}=\lambda(i+\hat{j}-3 \hat{k}){/tex}, for some scalar {tex}\lambda{/tex}.
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x{tex}\hat i{/tex} + (2x + y){tex}\hat j{/tex} + (2y - x){tex}\hat k{/tex} {tex}=\lambda(\hat{i}+\vec{j}-3 \hat{k}){/tex}
On comparing, we get
{tex}x=\lambda{/tex} ...(i)
{tex}2 x+y=\lambda{/tex} ...(ii)
2y - x = {tex}-3 \lambda{/tex} ...(iii)
From (i) and (ii), {tex}2 \lambda+y=\lambda {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = {tex}-\lambda{/tex}
x = {tex}\lambda{/tex} and y = {tex}-\lambda{/tex} satisfy (iii).
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{r}=\lambda(\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-\hat{k})-\lambda(\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{r}=\lambda(\hat{i}+\hat{j}-3 \hat{k}){/tex}, where {tex}\lambda \in{/tex} R
For {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 2,
{tex}\vec{r}=2 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-6 \hat{k}{/tex}

535316.explanation:
Let I = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac{1}{8}} \frac{d x}{1+(\cot 4 \pi x)^{\sqrt{2}}}{/tex}
Let 4{tex}\pi{/tex}x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = {tex}\frac{d t}{4 \pi}{/tex}
I = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\frac{d t}{4 \pi}}{1+(\cot t)^{\sqrt{2}}}{/tex}
I = {tex}\frac{1}{4 \pi} \int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{(\sin t)^{\sqrt{2}}}{(\sin t)^{\sqrt{2}}+(\cos t)^{\sqrt{2}}}{/tex}dt ...(i)
Replacing x by {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} - x, we get
I = {tex}\frac{1}{4 \pi} \int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{(\cos t)^{\sqrt{2}}}{(\cos t)^{\sqrt{2}}+(\sin t)^{\sqrt{2}}}{/tex}dt ...(ii)
Adding (i) and (ii), we get 2I = {tex}\frac{1}{4 \pi} \int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex}dt
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{1}{4 \pi}\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)=\frac{1}{16}{/tex}

535324.explanation:
Let l, m, n be the direction cosines.
Then l = cos {tex}\theta{/tex}, m = cos {tex}\beta{/tex} and n = cos {tex}\theta{/tex}
l2 + m2 + n2 = 1
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} cos2 {tex}\theta{/tex} + cos2 {tex}\beta{/tex} + cos2 {tex}\theta{/tex} = 1
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 2 cos2 {tex}\theta{/tex} + 1 - sin2 {tex}\beta{/tex} = 1
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 2 cos2 {tex}\theta{/tex} - sin2 {tex}\beta{/tex} = 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 2 cos2 {tex}\theta{/tex} - 3 sin2 {tex}\theta{/tex} = 0 ... [Given sin2{tex}\beta{/tex} = 3sin2{tex}\theta{/tex}]
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 2 cos2 {tex}\theta{/tex} - 3(1 - cos2 {tex}\theta{/tex}) = 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 5 cos2 {tex}\theta{/tex} - 3 = 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} cos2{tex}\theta{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3}{5}{/tex}

535332.explanation:
Let I = {tex}\int \limits_{1}^{4} \frac{2}{x} e^{\sin x^{2}}{/tex}dx = {tex}\int \limits_{1}^{4} \frac{2 x e^{\sin x^{2}}}{x^{2}}{/tex}dx
Let x2 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x dx = dt
I = {tex}\int \limits_{1}^{16} \frac{e^{\sin t}}{t}{/tex}dt
= {tex}\int \limits_{1}^{16} \frac{e^{\sin x}}{x}{/tex}dx
= {tex}[f(x)]_{1}^{16}{/tex}
= f(16) - f(1)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} k = 16

535340.explanation:
{tex}2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex}
Given that, {tex}\vec{a}=-\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}=-\hat{i}-\hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex}
Calculating the vector product, we get

{tex} \vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\left|\begin{array}{ccc} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ 0 & -1 & 1 \\ -1 & -1 & -1 \end{array}\right| {/tex}
{tex} =\hat{i}(1-(-1))-\hat{j}(0-(-1))+\hat{k}(0-1) {/tex}
{tex} =2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex}

535348.explanation:
Direction ratios of the line joining (1, -2, 4) to (-1, 1,-2) are proportional to -2, 3, - 6
cos {tex}\theta{/tex} = {tex}\frac{|(2)(-2)+(3)(3)+(6)(-6)|}{\sqrt{2^{2}+3^{2}+6^{2}} \sqrt{(-2)^{2}+3^{2}+(-6)^{2}}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{31}{40}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\theta{/tex} = {tex}\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{31}{49}\right){/tex}

535351.explanation:
{tex}\int \limits_{0}^{2 \pi} \sqrt{1+\sin \frac{x}{2}} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{2 \pi} \sqrt{\left(\sin \frac{x}{4}+\cos \frac{x}{4}\right)^{2}} d x{/tex}
= {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{2 \pi}\left(\sin \frac{x}{4}+\cos \frac{x}{4}\right) d x{/tex} ...{tex}\left[\frac{x}{4} \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right) \Rightarrow\left(\sin \frac{x}{4}+\cos \frac{x}{4}\right)>0\right]{/tex}
= {tex}4\left[-\cos \frac{x}{4}+\sin \frac{x}{4}\right]_{0}^{2 \pi}{/tex}
= 4(0 + 1 + 1 - 0)
= 8

535356.explanation:
The projection of {tex}\alpha \hat{i}+\beta \hat{j}{/tex} on {tex}3 \hat{i}-4 \hat{j}=1{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \frac{(\alpha \hat{i}+\beta \hat{j}) \cdot(3 \hat{i}-4 \hat{j})}{|3 \hat{i}-4 \hat{j}|}=1{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \frac{3 \alpha-4 \beta}{5}=1{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow 3 \alpha-4 \beta=5{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3(1 + {tex}2 \beta{/tex}) - {tex}4 \beta{/tex} = 5 ...[{tex}\alpha-2 \beta{/tex} = 1]
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 \beta{/tex} = 2 {tex}\Leftrightarrow \beta{/tex} = 1

535367.explanation:
{tex}\int \limits_{0}^{1} \frac{x^{4}(1-x)^{4}}{1+x^{2}} d x{/tex}
= {tex}\int_{0}^{1}\left(x^{6}-4 x^{5}+5 x^{4}-4 x^{2}+4-\frac{4}{1+x^{2}}\right) d x{/tex}
= {tex}\left[\frac{x^{7}}{7}-\frac{2 x^{6}}{3}+x^{5}-\frac{4}{3} x^{3}+4 x-4 \tan ^{-1} x\right]_{0}^{1}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{7}-\frac{2}{3}+1-\frac{4}{3}+4-4\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right){/tex}
= {tex}\frac{22}{7}-\pi{/tex}

535372.explanation:
Given vectors {tex}\vec a{/tex} and {tex}\vec b{/tex} are perpendicular to each other.
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=0{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow(\hat{i}-\lambda \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}) \cdot(3 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+5 \hat{k})=0{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 3 + 2{tex}\lambda{/tex} + 10 = 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 2{tex}\lambda{/tex} + 13 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow \lambda=-\frac{13}{2}{/tex}

535380.explanation:
I2 = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{1} \frac{x}{e^{x^{2}}\left(2-x^{2}\right)}{/tex}dx
Let x2 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x dx = {tex}\frac{d t}{2}{/tex} 
I2 = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int_{0}^{1} \frac{d t}{e^{t}(2-t)}{/tex}
Replacing t by 1 - 1, we get
I2 = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int \limits_{0}^{1} \frac{d t}{e^{1-t}(1+t)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2 e} \int \limits_{0}^{1} \frac{e^{t}}{1+t}{/tex}dt
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I2 = {tex}\frac{1}{2 e} \int \limits_{0}^{1} \frac{e^{x}}{1+x}{/tex}dx = {tex}\frac{1}{2 e}{/tex}I1
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{I}_{2}}{\mathrm{I}_{1}}=\frac{1}{2 e}{/tex}

535388.explanation:
l2 + m2 + n2 = 1
(l + m + n)2 = l2 + m2 + n2 + 2(Im + mn + nl)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 + 2 (ln + mn + nl) {tex}\ge{/tex} 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} lm + mn + nl {tex}\ge{/tex} {tex}-\frac{1}{2}{/tex} ... (i)
Using A.M. - G.M. property,
{tex}\frac{l^{2}+m^{2}}{2} \geq \sqrt{l^{2} m^{2}}, \frac{m^{2}+n^{2}}{2} \geq \sqrt{m^{2} n^{2}}, \frac{n^{2}+l^{2}}{2} \geq \sqrt{n^{2} l^{2}}{/tex}
Adding all, we get
2(l2 + m2 + n2) {tex}\ge{/tex} 2 (Im + mn + nl)
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} lm + mn + nl {tex}\le{/tex} 1 ... (ii)
(i) and (ii) give the range of the given expression = {tex}\left[-\frac{1}{2}, 1\right]{/tex}

535390.explanation:
Let I = {tex}\int \limits_{\frac{\pi}{3}}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sqrt{1+\cos x}}{(1-\cos x)^{\frac{3}{2}}} d x{/tex}
= {tex}\int \limits_{\frac{\pi}{3}}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sqrt{1+\cos x}}{(1-\cos x)^{\frac{5}{2}}} \times \frac{\sqrt{1-\cos x}}{\sqrt{1-\cos x}} d x{/tex}
= {tex}\int \limits_{\frac{\pi}{3}}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin x}{(1-\cos x)^{3}} d x{/tex}
Let 1 - cos x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} sin x dx = dt
When x = {tex}\frac {\pi}{3}{/tex}, t = {tex}\frac {1}{2}{/tex} and when x = {tex}\frac {\pi}{2}{/tex}, t = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int \limits_{\frac{1}{2}}^{1} \frac{d t}{t^{3}}=-\frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{1}{t^{2}}\right]_{\frac{1}{2}}^{1}=\frac{3}{2}{/tex}

535396.explanation:
Given, {tex}2 \vec{a}+3 \bar{b}=\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}{/tex} ...(i)
{tex}3 \vec{a}+2 \vec{b}=2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}{/tex} ...(ii)
From (i) and (ii), we get
{tex}\vec{a}=\frac{1}{5}(4 \hat{i}-7 \hat{j}){/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}=\frac{1}{5}(-\hat{i}+8 \hat{j}){/tex}
{tex}\cos \theta=\frac{\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}}{|\vec{a}||\vec{b}|}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{\frac{1}{5} \times \frac{1}{5}(-4-56)}{\frac{1}{5} \sqrt{65} \times \frac{1}{5} \sqrt{65}}=-\frac{12}{13}{/tex}

535398.explanation:
Let I = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\sqrt{\log \frac{\pi}{2}}} \cos \left(e^{x^{2}}\right) \cdot 2 x e^{x^{2}}{/tex} dx
Let {tex}e^{x^{2}}{/tex} = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x{tex}e^{x^{2}}{/tex} dx = dt
I = {tex}\int \limits_{1}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \cos t{/tex} dt = {tex}[\sin t]_{1}^{\pi / 2}{/tex}
= sin {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} - sin 1 = 1 - sin 1

535403.explanation:
N = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{\sin x \cos x}{(x+1)^{2}}{/tex}dx
= {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{\sin 2 x}{(x+1)^{2}}{/tex}dx
= {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\left[\sin 2 x\left(-\frac{1}{x+1}\right)\right]_{0}^{7 \frac{\pi}{4}}+\int_{e}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{2 \cos 2 x}{x+1} d x\right]{/tex}
= {tex}-\frac{2}{\pi+4}+\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{\cos 2 x}{x+1} d x{/tex}
In 2nd integral, let 2x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = {tex}\frac{d t}{2}{/tex}
N = {tex}-\frac{2}{\pi+4}+\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\cos t}{t+2}{/tex}dt
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} N = {tex}-\frac{2}{\pi+4}{/tex} + M
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} M - N = {tex}\frac{2}{\pi+4}{/tex}

535406.explanation:
Let I = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac{4}{\pi}}\left(2 x \sin \frac{1}{x}-\cos \frac{1}{x}\right){/tex}dx
Let x2 sin {tex}\frac{1}{x}{/tex} = t

535514.explanation:

and {tex}L_2{/tex} is parallel to the vector

{tex}\vec{V}_2=a \hat{i}+b \hat{j}+c \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \cos \alpha=\frac{\left|\overrightarrow{V_1} \cdot \overrightarrow{V_2}\right|}{\left|\overrightarrow{V_1}\right|\left|\overrightarrow{V_2}\right|}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{|a(\cos \theta+\sqrt{3})+(b \sqrt{2}) \sin \theta+c(\cos \theta-\sqrt{3})|}{\sqrt{a^2+b^2+c^2} \sqrt{(\cos \theta+\sqrt{3})^2+2 \sin ^2 \theta+(\cos \theta-\sqrt{3})^2}}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{|(a+c) \cos \theta+b \sqrt{2} \sin \theta+(a-c) \sqrt{3}|}{\sqrt{a^2+b^2+c^2} \sqrt{2+6}}{/tex}
For {tex}\cos \alpha{/tex} to be independent of {tex}\theta{/tex}, we get

{tex}a+c=0 \text { and } b=0{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \cos \alpha=\frac{2 a \sqrt{3}}{a \sqrt{2} 2 \sqrt{2}}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\text { or } \alpha=\frac{\pi}{6}{/tex}

Comparing with

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} The given line lies in xy plane.

535542.explanation:
Let {tex}a \hat{i}+b \hat{j}+c \hat{k}{/tex} be the vector in the new position. Since it is perpendicular to the given vector.
{tex}\Rightarrow(a \hat{i}+b \hat{j}+c \hat{k}) \cdot(7 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k})=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 7a + 4b + 4c = 0 ...(i)
Also,
{tex}|a \vec{i}+b \hat{j}+c \hat{k}|=|7 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k}|{/tex} ...[Magnitude remains the same]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a2 + b2 + c2 = 81 ...(ii)
The vector in a new position, given vector and x-axis are coplanar.
{tex}\Rightarrow\left|\begin{array}{lll} a & b & c \\ 7 & 4 & 4 \\ 1 & 0 & 0 \end{array}\right|=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1(4b - 4c) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} b = c ...(iii)
From (i) and (iii), we get
{tex}a=-\frac{8}{7} b{/tex} ...(iv)
From (ii),
{tex}\left(-\frac{8}{7} b\right)^{2}{/tex} + b2 + b2 = 81
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(\frac{64}{49}+2\right) b^{2}=81{/tex} {tex}\Leftrightarrow\left(\frac{162}{49}\right) b^{2}=81{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow b^{2}=\frac{49 \times 81}{162}=\frac{49}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow b=\pm \frac{7}{\sqrt{2}}=\frac{\pm 7 \sqrt{2}}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow a=-\frac{8}{7} b=-\frac{8}{7} \times\left(\pm \frac{7 \sqrt{2}}{2}\right){/tex} ...[From (iv)]
{tex}=\pm 4 \sqrt{2}{/tex}
It is given that vector passes through the positive x-axis.
{tex}\Rightarrow a=4 \sqrt{2}{/tex}
Substituting the value of a in (iv), we get
{tex}b=\frac{-7 \sqrt{2}}{2}=c{/tex}
Hence, vector in new position is
{tex}4 \sqrt{2} \hat{i}-7\left(\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}\right) \hat{j}-\frac{7 \sqrt{2}}{2} \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}=\sqrt{2}\left(4 \hat{i}-\frac{7}{2} \hat{j}-\frac{7}{2} \hat{k}\right){/tex}

535545.explanation:
{tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{BC}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{CA}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AB}}=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{o}}{/tex} ...[Triangle law]
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}=\vec{0}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a}=-\vec{b}-\vec{c}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \times \vec{b}{/tex} {tex}=-\vec{b} \times \vec{b}-\vec{c} \times \vec{b}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \times \vec{b}{/tex} {tex}=\vec{b} \times \vec{c}{/tex} ...(i)
Similarly,
{tex}\vec{b} \times \vec{c}=\bar{c} \times \vec{a}{/tex} ..(ii)
From (i) and (ii), we get
{tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\vec{b} \times \vec{c}=\vec{c} \times \bar{a}{/tex}

535548.explanation:
Direction ratios of AB are proportional to 4, -1, 1.
Direction ratios of CD are proportional to 0, -1, 2 - m.
Equation of AB is
{tex}\vec{r}_{1}=(6 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k})+\lambda(4 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+\hat{k}){/tex}
Equation of CD is
{tex}\vec{r}_{2}=(\hat{i}+5 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k})+m(-\hat{j}+(2-m) \hat{k}){/tex}
Let {tex}\vec{a_{1}}=6 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}, \vec{a_{2}}=\hat{i}+5 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\vec{b}_{1}=4 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+\hat{k}, \vec{b_{2}}=-\hat{j}+(2-m) \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{b}_{1} \times \vec{b_{2}}=\left|\begin{array}{ccc} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ 4 & -1 & 1 \\ 0 & -1 & 2-m \end{array}\right|{/tex}
= {tex}(m-1) \hat{i}-(8-4 m) \hat{j}+(-4) \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow{a_{2}}-\overrightarrow{a_{1}}=-5 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k}{/tex}
Shortest distance = {tex}\frac{\left|\left(\overrightarrow{a_{2}}-\bar{a}_{1}\right) \cdot\left(\bar{b}_{1} \times \vec{b}_{2}\right)\right|}{\left|\vec{b}_{1} \times \overrightarrow{b_{2}}\right|}{/tex}
3 = {tex}\frac{\mid-5(m-1)-2(8-4 m)-16}{\sqrt{(m-1)^{2}+(4 m-8)^{2}+16}}{/tex}
Squaring, we get
9[(m - 1)2 + 16 (m - 2)2 + 16] = (3m - 27)2
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 16m(m - 3) = 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} m = 3 ... [m > 0]

535551.explanation:
Let I = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{x \tan x}{\sec x+\tan x}{/tex}dx ...(i)
Replacing x by {tex}\pi{/tex} - x, we get
I = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{(\pi-x) \tan x}{\sec x+\tan x}{/tex}dx ...(ii)
Adding (i) and (ii), we get
2I = {tex}\pi \int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\tan x}{\sec x+\tan x}{/tex}dx
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2} \int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\sin x}{1+\sin x}{/tex}dx
= I1, say
= {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}[\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} 1 d x-\underbrace{\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{d x}{1+\sin x}}_{ }]{/tex} = I1 (say)
I1 = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{1-\sin x}{1-\sin ^{2} x}{/tex}dx
= {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\pi}{/tex}(sec2x - sec x tan x)dx
= {tex}[\tan x-\sec x]_{0}^{\pi}{/tex} = 0 - (-1 - 1) = 2
I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}({tex}\pi{/tex} - I1)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}(\pi-2){/tex} = {tex}\pi\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-1\right){/tex}

535555.explanation:
Given, {tex}\vec{b}=b_{1} \hat{i}+b_{2} \hat{j}+b_{3} \hat{k}{/tex}
and {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}+\vec{c}=\vec{o}{/tex} {tex}\Leftrightarrow \vec{b} \times \vec{a}=\vec{c}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow\left|\begin{array}{ccc} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \dot{k} \\ b_{1} & b_{2} & b_{3} \\ 1 & -1 & 0 \end{array}\right|{/tex} {tex}=2 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-\dot{k}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2}(\hat{i}+\hat{j}+4 \hat{k}){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\hat i{/tex}(0 + b3) - {tex}\hat j{/tex}(0 - b3) + {tex}\hat k{/tex}(-b1 - b2) {tex}=2 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} b3{tex}\hat i{/tex} + b3{tex}\hat j{/tex} - (b1 + b2){tex}\hat k{/tex} {tex}=2 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} b3 = 2 and b1 + b2 = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2b1 = 1 ...[b1 = b2]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} b1 = b2 = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{b}=\frac{1}{2} \hat{i}+\frac{1}{2} \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex} or {tex}\frac{1}{2}(\hat{i}+\hat{j}+4 \hat{k}){/tex}

535557.explanation:
f(x) = {tex}\frac{e^{x}}{1+e^{x}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f(a) + f(-a) = {tex}\frac{e^{a}}{1+e^{a}}+\frac{e^{-a}}{1+e^{-a}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f(a) + f(-a) = {tex}\frac{e^{a}}{1+e^{a}}+\frac{1}{1+e^{a}}{/tex} = 1
Using {tex}\int \limits_{a}^{b}{/tex}f(x)dx = {tex}\int \limits_{a}^{b}{/tex}f(a + b - x)dx, we have
I1 = {tex}\int \limits_{f(-a)}^{f(a)}{/tex}(1 - x)g(1 - x)x)dx ...[f(a) + f(-a) = 1]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I1 = {tex}\int \limits_{f(-a)}^{f(a)} {/tex}g(1 - x)x)dx - {tex}\int \limits_{f(-a)}^{f(a)} {/tex}xg(1 - x)x)dx
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I1 = I2 - I1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2I1 = I2 {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{I}_{2}}{\mathrm{I}_{1}}{/tex} = 2

535559.explanation:
Given, {tex}\vec{a}-3 \vec{b}+4 \vec{c}=\vec{0}{/tex} ...(i)
and {tex}3 \vec{a}-5 \vec{b}-8 \vec{c}=\vec{0}{/tex} ...(ii)
Multiplying (i) by 3 and subtracting from (ii), we get
{tex}-4 \vec{b}+20 \vec{c}=\vec{0}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \vec{b}=5 \vec{c}{/tex}
i.e. {tex}\vec b{/tex} and {tex}\vec c{/tex} are like parallel vectors {tex}\Rightarrow|\vec{b} \times \vec{c}|=0{/tex}

535563.explanation:
P'(x) = P'(1 - x)
Integrating on both sides, we get
P(x) = -P(1 - x) + c ... (i)
Substituting x = 0 in (i), we get
P(0) = -P(1 - 0) + c
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 = -41 + c {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} c = 42
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} P(x) + P(1 - x) = 42 ... [From (i)]
Let I = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{1} P(x){/tex}dx
Replacing x by 1 - x, we get
I = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{1} P{/tex}(1 - x)dx
= {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{1}{/tex}(42 - P(x))dx
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 21 = 42 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = 21

535567.explanation:
{tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \perp \bar{b}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a}=\vec{o}{/tex} or {tex}\vec{b}=\vec {o}{/tex}
and {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\vec{o}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \| \vec{b}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a}=\vec{o}{/tex} or {tex}\vec{b}=\vec{o}{/tex}
Hence, either {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} or {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} is null vector.

535571.explanation:
Applying L' Hospital's rule, we get
{tex}\lim \limits_{x \rightarrow \frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{\int \limits_{2}^{\sec ^{2} x} f(t) d t}{x^{2}-\frac{\pi^{3}}{16}}{/tex} = {tex}\lim \limits_{x \rightarrow \frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{2 \sec ^{2} x \tan x f\left(\sec ^{2} x\right)}{2 x}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{2 \times 2 \times 1 \times f(2)}{2 \times \frac{\pi}{4}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{8}{\pi}{/tex}f(2)

535573.explanation:
Given that, {tex}\bar{v} \times \vec{b}=\vec{c} \times \vec{b}{/tex} {tex}\Leftrightarrow(\vec{v}-\bar{c}) \times \vec{b}=\vec{0}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \vec{v}-\vec{c}{/tex} and {tex}\vec b{/tex} are collinear.
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \vec{v}-\vec{c}=\lambda \vec{b}, \lambda \neq 0{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \vec{v}=\vec{c}+\lambda \vec{b}{/tex} {tex}=4 \hat{i}-3 \hat{j}+7 \hat{k}+\lambda(\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}){/tex}
{tex}=(4+\lambda) \hat{i}+(-3+\lambda) \hat{j}+(7+\lambda) \hat{k}{/tex}
Also, {tex}\vec{v} \cdot \vec{a}=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2(4 + {tex}\lambda{/tex}) + (7 + {tex}\lambda{/tex}) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow \lambda{/tex} = -5
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{v}=\vec{c}-5 \bar{b}=-\hat{i}-8 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex}

535577.explanation:
f(x) = {tex}\int \limits_{1}^{x} \frac{\log t}{1+t}{/tex}dt
{tex}\Rightarrow f\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)=\int \limits_{1}^{\frac{1}{x}} \frac{\log t}{1+t}{/tex}dt
Let t = {tex}\frac{1}{u}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dt = {tex}-\frac{1}{u^{2}}{/tex}du
{tex}\Rightarrow f\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)=\int \limits_{1}^{x} \frac{\log \left(\frac{1}{u}\right)}{1+\frac{1}{u}} x-\frac{1}{u^{2}}{/tex}du
= {tex}\int \limits_{1}^{x} \frac{-\log \left(\frac{1}{u}\right)}{u(1+u)}{/tex}du
= {tex}\int \limits_{1}^{x} \frac{\log u}{u(1+u)}{/tex}du
= {tex}\int \limits_{1}^{x} \frac{\log t}{t(1+t)}{/tex}dt
F(x) = f(x) + f{tex}\left(\frac{1}{x}\right){/tex} ...[Given]
= {tex}\int \limits_{1}^{x} \frac{\log t}{1+t} d t+\int \limits_{1}^{x} \frac{\log t}{t(1+t)}{/tex}dt = {tex}\int \limits_{1}^{x} \frac{\log t}{t}{/tex}dt
= {tex}\left[\frac{(\log t)^{2}}{2}\right]_{1}^{x}=\frac{(\log x)^{2}}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} F(e) = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}

535579.explanation:
Given, {tex}\vec{b} \times \vec{c}=\vec{b} \times \vec{d}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \vec{b} \times(\vec{c}-\vec{d})=\vec{0}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \vec{b} \mid \vec{c}-\vec{d}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \vec{c}-\bar{d}=\lambda \vec{b}, \lambda \neq 0{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \vec{d}=\vec{c}-\lambda \vec{b}{/tex} ...(i)
and {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{d}=0{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \vec{a} \cdot(\bar{c}-\lambda \vec{b})=0{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}-\lambda(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}){/tex} {tex}\Leftrightarrow \lambda=\frac{\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}}{\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}}{/tex}
(i) {tex}\Rightarrow \vec{d}=\vec{c}-\left(\frac{\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}}{\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}}\right) \vec{b}{/tex}

535581.explanation:
Let I = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\sqrt{3}} \frac{\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x}{1+x^{2}}\right)}{1+x^{2}}{/tex}dx
Let t = tan-1x, 0 < x < {tex}\sqrt 3{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = tan t, 0 < t < {tex}\frac {\pi}{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = sec2t dt
I = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac {\pi}{3}} \frac{\sin ^{-1}\left({sin 2t}\right)}{sec^{2}t}{/tex}sec2t dt
I = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{3}} \sin ^{-1}(\sin 2 t) d t{/tex}...[I {tex}\neq{/tex} {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac {\pi}{3}} {/tex}2t dt as 0 < 2t < {tex}\frac {2\pi}{3}{/tex}]
= {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac {\pi}{4}} {/tex}2t dt + {tex}\int \limits_{\frac {\pi}{4}}^{\frac {\pi}{3}} {/tex}({tex}\pi{/tex} - 2t)dt ...{tex}\left[ {\frac{\pi }{2} < 2t < \frac{{2\pi }}{3}} \right.{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left. {\frac{\pi }{3} < \pi - 2t < \frac{\pi }{2}} \right]{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{{{\pi ^2}}}{{16}} + \pi \left( {\frac{\pi }{3} - \frac{\pi }{4}} \right) - \left( {\frac{{{\pi ^2}}}{9}\frac{{{\pi ^2}}}{{16}}} \right){/tex}
= {tex} \frac{{{\pi ^2}}}{8} + \frac{{2{\pi ^2}}}{9} - \frac{{{\pi ^2}}}{4}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{{9{\pi ^2} + 16{\pi ^2} - 18{\pi ^2}}}{{72}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac {7\pi^2}{72}{/tex}

535584.explanation:
f(x) = 2x3 - 21x2 + 78x + 24
{tex}f^{\prime}{/tex}(x) = 6(x2 - 7x + 13)
{tex}\Rightarrow f^{\prime}{/tex}(x) > 0 {tex}\forall{/tex} x {tex}\in{/tex} R
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f(x) is increasing function
Now, f(f(f(x) - 2x3)) {tex}\geq{/tex} f(f(2x3 - f(x)))
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f(f(x) - 2x3) {tex}\geq{/tex} f(2x3 - f(x)) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f(x) - 2x3 {tex}\geq{/tex} 2x3 - f(x)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f(x) {tex}\geq{/tex} 2x3 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 7x2 - 26x - 8 {tex}\leq{/tex} 0
{tex}\Rightarrow x \in\left[-\frac{2}{7}, 4\right]{/tex}

535615.explanation:
{tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}|\sin x-\cos x|{/tex}dx
= {tex}-\int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}(\sin x-\cos x){/tex}dx + {tex}\int \limits_{\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(\sin x-\cos x){/tex}dx
= {tex}-[-\cos x-\sin x]_{0}^{\pi / 4}{/tex} + {tex}[-\cos x-\sin x]_{\pi / 4}^{\pi / 2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{2}{\sqrt{2}}-1+\frac{2}{\sqrt{2}}-1{/tex} = 2({tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} - 1)

535623.explanation:
{tex}\int \limits_{0}^{100}[\sqrt{x}] d x{/tex}
= {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{1}[\sqrt{x}] d x+\int \limits_{1}^{4}[\sqrt{x}] d x{/tex} + {tex}9 \int \limits_{4}^{9}[\sqrt{x}] d x+\int \limits_{9}^{16}[\sqrt{x}] d x{/tex} +...+ {tex}\int \limits_{64}^{81}[\sqrt{x}] d x+\int \limits_{81}^{100}[\sqrt{x}] d x{/tex}
= {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{1} 0 \cdot d x+\int \limits_{1}^{4} 1 \cdot d x{/tex} + {tex}\int_{4}^{9} 2 d x+\int_{9}^{16} 3 d x{/tex} +...+ {tex}\int \limits_{64}^{81} 8 d x+\int \limits_{81}^{100} 9 d x{/tex}
= 3 + 2(5) + 3(7) + 4(9) + 5(11) + 6(13) + 7(15) + 8(17) + 9(19)
= 615

535628.explanation:
{tex}\int \limits_{\pi}^{2 \pi}[\sqrt{2} \cos x]{/tex}dx = {tex}\int \limits_{\pi}^{\frac{5 \pi}{4}}[\sqrt{2} \cos x]{/tex}dx + {tex}\int \limits_{\frac{5 \pi}{4}}^{\frac{3 \pi}{2}}[\sqrt{2} \cos x]{/tex}dx + {tex}\int \limits_{\frac{3 \pi}{2}}^{\frac{7 \pi}{4}}[\sqrt{2} \cos x]{/tex}dx + {tex}\int \limits_{\frac{7 \pi}{4}}^{2 \pi}[\sqrt{2} \cos x]{/tex}dx
= {tex}\int \limits_{\pi}^{\frac{5 \pi}{4}}(-2){/tex}dx + {tex}\int \limits_{\frac{5 \pi}{4}}^{\frac{3 \pi}{2}}(-1){/tex}dx + {tex}\int \limits_{\frac{3 \pi}{2}}^{\frac{7 \pi}{4}} 0 . d x{/tex} + {tex}\int_{\frac{7 \pi}{4}}^{2 \pi} 1 . d x{/tex}
= {tex}-2\left(\frac{5 \pi}{4}-\pi\right)-1\left(\frac{3 \pi}{2}-\frac{5 \pi}{4}\right){/tex}{tex}+\left(2 \pi-\frac{7 \pi}{4}\right){/tex}
= {tex}-\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}

535639.explanation:
f(x) = tan x, {tex}0 \leq x \leq \frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}
= cot x, {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}<x \leq \frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} f(x){/tex}dx = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \tan x{/tex}dx + {tex}\int \limits_{\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \cot x{/tex} dx
= {tex}[\log |\sec x|]_{0}^{\pi / 4}{/tex} + {tex}[\log |\sin x|]_{\pi / 4}^{\pi / 2}{/tex}
= {tex}(\log \sqrt{2}-0)+\left(0-\log \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right){/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}log 2 + {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}log 2 = log 2

535647.explanation:
Let I = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{2^{\sin x}}{2^{\sin x}+2^{\cos x}}{/tex}dx ...(i)
Replacing x by {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}-x{/tex}, we get
I = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{2^{\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right)}}{2^{\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right)}+2^{\cos \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right)}}{/tex}dx ...{tex}\left[\because \int \limits_{0}^{a} f(x) d x=\int \limits_{0}^{a} f(a-x) d x\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{2^{\cos x}}{2^{\cos x}+2^{\sin x}}{/tex}dx ...(ii)
Adding (i) and (ii), we get
2I = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} d x=[x]_{0}^{\pi / 2}=\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}

535656.explanation:
Let I = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} x f(\sin x){/tex}dx ...(i)
Replacing x by {tex}\pi{/tex} - x, we get
I = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\pi}(\pi-x){/tex} f(sin({tex}\pi{/tex} - x))dx ...{tex}\left[\because \int \limits_{0}^{a} f(x) d x=\int \limits_{0}^{a} f(a-x) d x\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\pi}(\pi-x){/tex} f(sin x)dx ...(ii)
Adding (i) and (ii), we get
2I = {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi} \pi{/tex} f(sin x)dx
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2} \int \limits_{0}^{\pi} f(\sin x){/tex}dx

535664.explanation:
Let I = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{3} \frac{1}{1+2^{f(x)}}{/tex}dx ...(i)
Replacing x by 3 - x, we get
I = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{3} \frac{1}{1+2^{f(3-x)}}{/tex}dx
= {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{3} \frac{1}{1+2^{-f(x)}}{/tex}dx
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{3} \frac{2^{f(x)}}{2^{f(x)}+1}{/tex} ...(ii)
Adding (i) and (ii), we get
2I = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{3} d x=[x]_{0}^{3}{/tex} = 3 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{3}{2}{/tex}

535668.explanation:
Let I = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \sin 2 x{/tex} log tan x dx ...(i)
Replacing x by {tex}\frac {\pi}{2}{/tex} - x, we get
I = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \sin (\pi-2 x){/tex}log cot x dx
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \sin 2 x{/tex} log cot x dx ...(ii)
Adding (i) and (ii), we get
2I = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \sin 2 x{/tex}(log(tan x cot x))dx
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = 0

535679.explanation:
Let I = {tex}\int \limits_{2}^{3} \frac{\left[x^{2}\right]}{\left[x^{2}\right]+\left[x^{2}-10 x+25\right]}{/tex}dx
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int \limits_{2}^{3} \frac{\left[x^{2}\right]}{\left[x^{2}\right]+\left[(5-x)^{2}\right]}{/tex}dx ...(i)

535701.explanation:

= 0

535772.explanation:
a = xyp-1, b = xyq-1, c = xyr-1
where x : first term, y : common ratio
Let the given vectors be {tex}\vec{\mathrm{X}}, \vec{\mathrm{Y}}{/tex} .
Let the angle between the vectors be {tex}\theta{/tex}. Then
(q - r) log a + (r - p) log b + (p - q) log c = {tex}|\vec{\mathrm{X}}||\vec{\mathrm{Y}}|{/tex} cos {tex}\theta{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow|\vec{\mathrm{X}} \| \vec{\mathrm{Y}}| \cos \theta{/tex} = (q - r) log (xyp - 1) + (r - p) log (xyq - 1) + (p - q) log (xyr - 1)
= log x[q - r + r - p + p - q] + (log y)[(p - 1)(q - r) + (q - 1)(r - p) + (r - 1)(p - q)]
= (0)logx + (0)log y = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow \cos \theta=0 \Rightarrow \theta=\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{r}=6 \vec{a}-4 \vec{b}+4 \vec{c}+\lambda(-6 \vec{a}+4 \vec{b}-8 \vec{c}){/tex} ... (i)
Equation of line CD in vector form is
{tex}\overrightarrow{r^{\prime}}=\vec{a}+2 \vec{b}-5 \vec{c}+\lambda^{\prime}(-\vec{a}-2 \vec{b}-3 \vec{c}-\{\vec{a}+2 \vec{b}-5 \vec{c}\}){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{r}^{\prime}=\vec{a}+2 \vec{b}-5 \vec{c}+\lambda^{\prime}(-2 \vec{a}-4 \vec{b}+2 \vec{c}){/tex} ... (ii)
The point of intersection of AB and CD will satisfy
{tex}\vec{r}=\vec{r}^{\prime}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}6 \vec{a}-4 \vec{b}+4 \vec{c}+\lambda(-6 \vec{a}+4 \vec{b}-8 \vec{c}){/tex} = {tex}\vec{a}+2 \vec{b}-5 \vec{c}+\lambda^{\prime}(-2 \vec{a}-4 \vec{b}+2 \vec{c}){/tex}
Comparing the coefficients of a and {tex}\vec b{/tex}, we get
6{tex}\lambda{/tex} - 2{tex}\lambda '{/tex} = 5 ... (iii)
2{tex}\lambda{/tex} + 2{tex}\lambda '{/tex} = 3 ... (iv)
Solving (iii) and (iv), we get
{tex}\lambda{/tex} = 1 and {tex}\lambda^{\prime}=\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
Substituting the value of {tex}\lambda{/tex} in (i), we get the point of intersection {tex}\vec{r}=-4 \vec{c}{/tex} i.e. point B.

535849.explanation:
To determine whether vector a and b are linearly dependent for the given intervals, we can use the fact that if a and b are linearly dependent, then there exist constant c1 and c2, not both zero, such that
c1a + c2b = 0
Now express a and b in terms of f(x), f'(x), g(x) g'(x)
{tex}\vec{a}{/tex} = f(x){tex}\hat{\imath}{/tex} + f'(x){tex}\hat{j}{/tex}
{tex}\vec{b}{/tex} = g(x){tex}\hat{\imath}{/tex} + g'(x){tex}\hat{j}{/tex}
Now, lets sot up the linear dependence eq.
c1(f(x)i - f'(x)j) + c2(g(x)i + g'(x)j = 0
Now separating the component.
c1f(x) + c2g(x) = 0
-c1f'(x) + c2g'(x) = 0.
integrate both sides of these eq. w.r.t. x.
c1{tex}\int {/tex}f(x)dx + c2{tex}\int {/tex}g(x)dx = k1
-c1{tex}\int {/tex}f'(x)dx + c2{tex}\int {/tex}g'(x)dx = k2
given that h(x) is antiderivative of f(x)g(x).
{tex}\int {/tex}f(x)g(x)dx = h(x) + k3
Given,
h(1) = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} k3 + k1 = 1 ...(i)
h(2) = 3 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} k3 + k2 = 3 ...(ii)
h(4) = 7 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} k3 + k4 = 7 ...(iii)
from (i) and (ii) (iii) and (i)
k2 - k1 = 2
k4 - k1 = 6
k2 = 2 + k1
k4 = k1 + 6.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} at least two x in (1, 4)
for x {tex}\in{/tex} (1, 4) k4 - k1 = 6 hold true.

= {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{1}\left[\frac{\pi}{2}-\tan ^{-1}\left(1-x+x^{2}\right)\right]{/tex}dx

535881.explanation:
Let the given position vectors be of points P, Q and R respectively, then
{tex}\left| {\vec{{\text{PQ}}} } \right| = \sqrt {{{(q - p)}^2} + {{(r - q)}^2} + {{(p - r)}^2}}{/tex}
{tex}\left| {\vec{{\text{QR}}} } \right| = \sqrt {{{(r - q)}^2} + {{(p - r)}^2} + {{(q - p)}^2}} {/tex}
{tex}\left| {\vec{RP} } \right| = \sqrt {{{(p - r)}^2} + {{(q - p)}^2} + {{(r - q)}^2}} {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \left| {\vec{PQ} } \right| = \left| {\vec{{\text{QR}}} } \right| = \left| {\vec{{\text{RP}}} } \right|{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \Delta{/tex}PQR is an equilateral triangle.

535896.explanation:
Let I = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{\sin x+\cos x}{9+16 \sin 2 x}{/tex}dx
= {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{\sin x+\cos x}{9+16\left[1-(\sin x-\cos x)^{2}\right]}{/tex}dx
Let sin x - cos x = t
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (cos x + sin x)dx = dt
I = {tex}\int \limits_{-1}^{0} \frac{d t}{9+16\left(1-t^{2}\right)}{/tex}
= {tex}\int \limits_{-1}^{0} \frac{1}{25-16 t^{2}}{/tex}dt
= {tex}\int \limits_{-1}^{0} \frac{1}{(5-4 t)(5+4 t)}{/tex}dt
= {tex}\int \limits_{-1}^{0}\left[\frac{\frac{1}{10}}{5-4 t}+\frac{\frac{1}{10}}{5+4 t}\right]{/tex}dt
= {tex}\frac{1}{10}\left[-\frac{1}{4} \log (5-4 t)+\frac{1}{4} \log (5+4 t)\right]_{-1}^{0}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{40}{/tex}(log 9 - log 1) = {tex}\frac{1}{20}{/tex}log 3

535919.explanation:

{tex}\Rightarrow 3 \vec{i}-\hat{k}{/tex} = (m2 + 2n){tex}\hat i{/tex} + (mn - mn){tex}\hat j{/tex} + (-2m + n2){tex}\hat k{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} m2 + 2n = 3 ...(i)
and -2m + n2 = -1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2m - n2 = 1 ...(ii)
From (i), {tex}n=\frac{3-m^{2}}{2}{/tex}
Substituting the value of n in (ii), we get
{tex}2 m-\left(\frac{3-m^{2}}{2}\right)^{2}=1{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} m4 - 6m2 - 8m + 13 = 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} (m - 1)(m3 + m2 - 5m - 13) = 0

535925.explanation:
{tex}\vec b_1{/tex} is parallel to {tex}\vec a{/tex}.
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec b_1=\lambda \vec a, \lambda \ne 0{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \vec b_1 = \lambda(\hat i + \hat j){/tex}
Given, {tex}\vec b_2 = \vec b - \vec b_1{/tex}
{tex}= (3\hat j + 4 \hat k) - \lambda(\hat i + \hat j){/tex}
{tex}=-\lambda\hat i+(3 - \lambda)\hat j+4\hat k{/tex}
Also, {tex}\vec a \cdot\vec b_2 = 0{/tex} ...[as {tex}\vec b_2{/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec a{/tex}]
{tex}\Leftrightarrow -\lambda + (3 - \lambda) = 0{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \lambda=\frac{3}{2}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow {\vec b_1} = \frac{3}{2}(\hat i + \hat j){/tex} and {tex}{\vec b_2} = \frac{3}{2}\hat i + \frac{3}{2}\hat j + 4\hat k{/tex}
{tex}{\vec b_2} \times {\vec b_2} = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\hat i}&{\hat j}&{\hat k} \\ {\frac{3}{2}}&{\frac{3}{2}}&0 \\ { - \frac{3}{2}}&{\frac{3}{2}}&4 \end{array}} \right|{/tex}
{tex}= \hat k\left( {\frac{9}{4} + \frac{9}{4}} \right) + 4\left( {\frac{3}{2}\hat i - \frac{3}{2}\hat j} \right){/tex}
{tex}=6\hat i - 6\hat j + \frac{9}{2}\hat k{/tex}

535928.explanation:
Let I = {tex}\int\limits_0^1 {\frac{{\log (1 + x)}}{{1 + {x^2}}}} {/tex}dx
Let x = tan {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = sec2{tex}\theta{/tex} d{tex}\theta{/tex}
I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{4}} {\frac{{\log (1 + \tan \theta )}}{{{{\sec }^2}\theta }}} \cdot {\sec ^2}\theta {/tex} d{tex}\theta{/tex}
Replacing {tex}\theta{/tex} by {tex}\frac{\pi }{4} - \theta {/tex}, we get
I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{4}} {\log \left( {1 + \frac{{1 + \tan \theta }}{{1 + \tan \theta }}} \right)} d\theta {/tex}
= {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{4}} {\log \left( {\frac{2}{{1 + \tan \theta }}} \right)} d\theta {/tex}
= log 2{tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{4}} {d\theta } - \underbrace {\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{4}} {\log (1 + \tan \theta )d\theta } }_{ = I}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2I = (log 2){tex}\left( {\frac{\pi }{4}} \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac {\pi}{8}{/tex}log 2

536009.explanation:
0 < x < 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex} > x2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 + {tex}x^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex} > 1 + x2
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{1+x^{\frac{\pi}{2}}}<\frac{1}{1+x^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \limits_{0}^{1} \frac{1}{1+x^{\frac{\pi}{2}}} d x<\left[\tan ^{-1} x\right]_{0}^{1}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}

{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} I > log 2

536011.explanation:
Given that: {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}-\hat{j}+3 \hat{k}, \vec{b}=5 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}=\hat{i}-\hat{j}{/tex}
Also given, {tex}\vec{a}+\mu \vec{b}{/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec{c}{/tex}
Therefore, {tex}(\vec{a}+\mu \vec{b}) \cdot \vec{c}=0{/tex}
i.e. {tex}(\hat{i}-\hat{j}+3 \hat{k}+\mu(5 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+\hat{k})) .(\hat{i}-\hat{j})=0{/tex}
{tex}((1+5 \mu) \hat{i}-(1+2 \mu) \hat{j}+(\mu+3) \hat{k}) \cdot(\hat{i}-\hat{j})=0{/tex}
{tex}1+5 \mu+1+2 \mu=0{/tex}
{tex}\mu=-\frac{7}{2}{/tex}.

536013.explanation:
{tex}(\hat i+\hat{j}+3 \hat{k}) x+(3 \hat{i}-3 \hat{j}+\hat{k}) y+(-4 \hat{i}+5 \hat{j}) z{/tex} {tex}=\lambda(x \hat{i}+y \hat{j}+z \hat{k}){/tex},
Comparing the coefficient of {tex}\hat{i}, \hat{j}, \hat{k}{/tex}, we get
(1 - {tex}\lambda{/tex})x + 3y - 4z = 0 ...(i)
x - (3 + {tex}\lambda{/tex})y + 5z = 0 ...(ii)
3x + y - {tex}\lambda{/tex}z = 0 ...(iii)
The equations (i), (ii) and (iii) will have a nontrivial solution if
{tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} 1-\lambda & 3 & -4 \\ 1 & -(3+\lambda) & 5 \\ 3 & 1 & -\lambda \end{array}\right|=0{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow(1-\lambda)\{\lambda(3+\lambda)-5\}-3\{-\lambda-15\}{/tex} - 4{1 + 3({tex}\lambda{/tex} + 3)) = 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow(1-\lambda)\left\{\lambda^{2}+3 \lambda-5\right\}-3\{-\lambda-15\}{/tex} - 4{3{tex}\lambda{/tex} + 10} = 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \lambda^{3}+2 \lambda^{2}+\lambda=0{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \lambda\left(\lambda^{2}+2 \lambda+1\right)=0{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \lambda(\lambda+1)^{2}=0 \Leftrightarrow \lambda=0{/tex} or -1

536015.explanation:
{tex}\lim \limits_{n \rightarrow \infty}\left(\frac{1}{n^{2}}+\frac{2}{n^{2}}+\frac{3}{n^{2}}+\ldots+\frac{n-1}{n^{2}}\right){/tex} = {tex}\lim \limits_{n \rightarrow \infty} \sum \limits_{r=1}^{n-1} \frac{r}{n^{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\lim \limits_{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{1}{n} \sum \limits_{r=1}^{n-1} \frac{r}{n}{/tex}
= {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{1} x d x=\left[\frac{x^{2}}{2}\right]_{0}^{1}=\frac{1}{2}{/tex}

536017.explanation:
Let f(x) = x3 - 3x2 + 5
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f'(x) = 3x2 - 6x = 3x (x - 2)
In (2, 3), f'(x) > 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f is increasing
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f(2) < f(x) < f(3)
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{\sqrt{f(2)}}>\frac{1}{\sqrt{f(x)}}>\frac{1}{\sqrt{f(3)}}{/tex} ...(i)
f(2 ) = 1, f(3) = 5, and (i) give
{tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{5}}<\frac{1}{\sqrt{f(x)}}<1{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I} \in\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{5}}, 1\right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 + {tex}\sqrt 3{/tex} > 2 and 1 + {tex}\sqrt 3{/tex} < 3
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} [1 + {tex}\sqrt 3{/tex}] = 2

536019.explanation:
{tex}\vec{r}=3 \vec{p}+4 \vec{q}{/tex} ...(i)
{tex}2 \vec{r}=\vec{p}-3 \vec{q}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 6 \vec{r}=3 \vec{p}-9 \vec{q}{/tex} ...(ii)
From (i) and (ii), we get
{tex}\vec{r}-4 \vec{q}=6 \vec{r}+9 \vec{q}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow-5 \vec{r}=13 \vec{q}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{r}=-\frac{13}{5} \vec{q}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow |\vec{r}|>2|\vec{q}|{/tex} and {tex}\vec{r}, \vec{q}{/tex} have opposite directions

536021.explanation:
Slope of angle bisector = tan 45° = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Equation of angle bisector is y = x
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} x - y = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Distance of {tex}C(2 \sqrt{2},-\sqrt{2}){/tex} from angle bisector of {tex}\vec{\mathrm{OA}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{OB}}{/tex} {tex}=\left|\frac{2 \sqrt{2}-(-\sqrt{2})}{\sqrt{1^{2}+1^{2}}}\right|{/tex} {tex}=\frac{3 \sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{2}}{/tex} = 3 units

536023.explanation:
Here, f(x) and cos {tex}\pi{/tex}x both are periodic with period 2.
Also, cos {tex}\pi{/tex}x is an even function.
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\int\limits_{ - 10}^{10} {f(x)} {/tex}cos {tex}\pi{/tex}x dx = 2{tex}\int \limits_{0}^{10} f(x){/tex}cos{tex}\pi{/tex}x dx
= 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{2} f(x){/tex}cos{tex}\pi{/tex}x dx
= 10{tex}\left\{\int \limits_{0}^{1}(1-x) \cos \pi x\right.{/tex} dx + {tex}\left.\int \limits_{1}^{2}(x-1) \cos \pi x d x\right\}{/tex}
= 10{tex}\left[(1-x) \cdot \frac{\sin \pi x}{\pi}\right]_{0}^{1}-10\left[\frac{\cos \pi x}{\pi^{2}}\right]_{0}^{1}{/tex} + 10{tex}\left[(x-1) \cdot \frac{\sin \pi x}{\pi}\right]_{1}^{2}-10\left[\frac{\cos \pi x}{\pi^{2}}\right]_{1}^{2}{/tex}
= 10{tex}\left[0-\frac{1}{\pi^{2}}(-2)+0+\frac{1}{\pi^{2}}(2)\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{40}{\pi^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\pi^{2}}{10} \int \limits_{-10}^{10} f(x){/tex}cos {tex}\pi{/tex}x dx = {tex}\frac{\pi^{2}}{10} \times \frac{40}{\pi^{2}}{/tex} = 4

536025.explanation:
Sum of given vectors {tex}=(\hat{i}+\hat{j}+3 \hat{k})+(3 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}-\hat{k}) {/tex} {tex}+(2 \hat{i}-3 \hat{j}-5 \hat{k}) {/tex}
{tex}=6 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-3 \hat{k}{/tex}
Required unit vector {tex}=\frac{6 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-3 \hat{k}}{|6 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-3 \hat{k}|}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{6 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-3 \hat{k}}{\sqrt{36+4+9}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{7}(6 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-3 \hat{k}){/tex}

536027.explanation:
We have of find {tex}(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AD}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{BE}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{CF}}){/tex}

Let the vertices A, B, C have position vectors {tex}\vec{a}, b, \vec{c} {/tex}. Then the midpoints D, E, F or BC, CA, AB have position vectors.
{tex}\frac{\vec{b}+\vec{c}}{2}, \frac{\vec{c}+\vec{a}}{2}, \frac{\vec{a}+\vec{b}}{2}{/tex} respectively.
{tex}\vec{\mathrm{AD}}+\vec{\mathrm{BE}}+\vec{\mathrm{CF}}{/tex} {tex}=\left(\frac{\vec{b}+\vec{c}}{2}-\vec{a}\right)+\left(\frac{\vec{c}+\vec{a}}{2}-\vec{b}\right)+\left(\frac{\vec{a}+\vec{b}}{2}-\vec{c}\right){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2}(\vec{b}+\vec{c}-2 \vec{a}+\vec{c}+\vec{a}-2 \vec{b}+\vec{a}+\vec{b}-2 \vec{c}){/tex}
{tex}=\vec 0{/tex}

536029.explanation: In the reverse integral property the upper limits and lower limits are interchanged. The reverse integral property of definite integrals is {tex}\int_a^b{/tex} {tex}{f}({x}) {dx}=-\int_b^a {f}({x}) {dx}{/tex}.

536033.explanation: {tex}\int_a^b {f}({x}) {dx}+\int_b^c({x}) {dx}=\int_a^c {f}({x}) {dx}{/tex} is a property of definite integrals. {tex}\int_a^b {f}({x}) {dx}+\int_b^c {f}({x}) {dx}=\int_a^c {f}({x}) {dx}{/tex} is called as adding intervals property used to combine a lower limit and upper limit of two different integrals.

536035.explanation:
{tex}\vec{c}=\lambda(\hat{a}+\hat{b}), \lambda>0{/tex} ...[{tex}\hat{a}+\hat{b}{/tex} is along a bisector of the angle between {tex}\vec a{/tex} and {tex}\vec b{/tex}]
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{c}=\lambda\left(\frac{\hat{i}-2 \hat{j}-3 \hat{k}}{\sqrt{1^{2}+2^{2}+3^{2}}}+\frac{-2 \hat{i}-3 \hat{j}+\hat{k}}{\sqrt{2^{2}+3^{2}+1^{2}}}\right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{c}=\lambda\left(\frac{-\hat{i}-5 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}}{\sqrt{14}}\right){/tex} ...(i)
{tex}\Rightarrow|\vec{c}|=|\lambda| \sqrt{\frac{1+25+4}{14}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow|\vec{c}|=\lambda \sqrt{\frac{15}{7}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \sqrt{30}=\lambda \sqrt{\frac{15}{7}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \lambda=\sqrt{14}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{c}=-\hat{i}-5 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex} ...[From (i)]
Also {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}=(\hat{i}-2 \hat{j}-3 \hat{k})+(-2 \hat{i}-3 \hat{j}+\hat{k}){/tex}
{tex}=-\hat{i}-5 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{c}=\vec{a}+\vec{b}{/tex}

536037.explanation:
{tex}\vec{p}+\vec{q}=\lambda_{1} \vec{r}{/tex} for some scalar {tex}\lambda_1{/tex} ...(i)
and {tex}\vec{q}+\vec{r}=\lambda_{2} \vec{p}{/tex} for some scalar {tex}\lambda_{2}{/tex} ...(ii)
From (i), we get
{tex}\vec{p}=\lambda_{1} \vec{r}-\vec{q}{/tex}
Substituting the value of {tex}\vec{p}{/tex} in (ii), we get
{tex}\vec{q}+\vec{r}=\lambda_{2}\left(\lambda_{1} \vec{r}-\vec{q}\right){/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \vec{q}+\vec{r}=\lambda_{1} \lambda_{2} \vec{r}-\lambda_{2} \vec{q}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow\left(1+\lambda_{2}\right) \vec{q}+\left(1-\lambda_{1} \lambda_{2}\right) \vec{r}=\vec{0}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow 1+\lambda_{2}=0{/tex} and {tex}1-\lambda_{1} \lambda_{2}=0{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \lambda_{2}=-1, \lambda_{1}=-1{/tex}
From (i),
{tex}\vec{p}+\vec{q}=(-1) \vec{r} \Rightarrow \vec{p}+\vec{q}+\vec{r}=\vec{0}{/tex}

536038.explanation:
We have,
{tex}\vec{\mathrm{AC}}=\vec{\mathrm{AB}}+\vec{\mathrm{BC}}=\vec{\mathrm{AB}}+\vec{\mathrm{AD}}{/tex} ...(i)
Let {tex}\vec{\mathrm{AB}}=x \vec{\mathrm{AP}}, \vec{\mathrm{AD}}=y \vec{\mathrm{AQ}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{AC}}=\vec{\mathrm{AR}}{/tex} ...(ii)

{tex}\Rightarrow|\vec{\mathrm{AB}}|=x|\vec{\mathrm{AP}}|,|\vec{\mathrm{AD}}|=y|\vec{\mathrm{AQ}}|{/tex} and {tex}|\vec{A C}|=z|\vec{A R}|{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x=\frac{|\vec{\mathrm{AB}}|}{|\vec{\mathrm{AP}}|}, y=\frac{\mid \vec{\mathrm{AD}}}{|\vec{\mathrm{AQ}}|} \text { and } z=\frac{\mid \vec{\mathrm{AC}}}{|\vec{\mathrm{AR}}|}{/tex}
From (i) and (ii), we get
{tex}z \vec{\mathrm{AR}}=x \vec{\mathrm{AP}}+y \vec{\mathrm{AQ}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x \vec{\mathrm{AP}}+y \vec{\mathrm{AQ}}-z \vec{\mathrm{AR}}=\vec{{0}}{/tex}
Since, points P, Q, R are collinear, the sum of the coefficients of their position vectors must be zero.
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + y - z = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + y = z
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{|\vec{\mathrm{AB}}|}{|\vec{\mathrm{AP}}|}+\frac{|\vec{\mathrm{AD}}|}{|\vec{\mathrm{AQ}}|}=\frac{|\vec{\mathrm{AC}}|}{|\vec{\mathrm{AR}}|}{/tex}

536041.explanation:
Vectors {tex}\vec V{/tex} can be given as,
{tex}\vec V_1{/tex} = (1, 1, 1), {tex}\vec V_5{/tex} = (-1, -1, -1) = {tex}\vec {V^{\prime}_1}{/tex} (say)
{tex}\vec V_2{/tex} = (-1, 1, 1), {tex}\vec V_6{/tex} = (1, -1, -1) = {tex}\vec {V'_2}{/tex}
{tex}\vec V_3{/tex} = (1, -1, 1), {tex}\vec V_7{/tex} = (-1, 1, -1) = {tex}\vec {V'_3}{/tex}
{tex}\vec V_4{/tex} = (1, 1, -1), {tex}\vec V_8{/tex} = (-1, -1, 1) = {tex}\vec {V'_4}{/tex}
Here, {tex}\vec V_1{/tex} and {tex}\vec {V'_1}{/tex} with any of the remaining six will produce a set of three coplanar vectors.
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Total number of ways of selecting a set of three coplanar vectors = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 24
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} The number of ways of selecting three non-coplanar vectors = 8C3 - 24 = 56 - 24 = 32

536043.explanation:
{tex}\lambda \hat{i}+\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}=\alpha(\hat{i}+\lambda \hat{j}-\hat{k})+\beta(2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+\lambda \hat{k}){/tex}
{tex}=(\alpha+2 \beta) \hat{i}+(\lambda \alpha-\beta) \hat{j}+(-\alpha+\lambda \beta) \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \lambda=\alpha+2 \beta{/tex} ...(i)
{tex}1=\lambda \alpha-\beta{/tex} ...(ii)
{tex}2=-\alpha+\lambda \beta{/tex} ...(ii)
Solving (ii) and (iii), we get
{tex}\alpha=\frac{\lambda+2}{\lambda^{2}-1}, \beta=\frac{2 \lambda+1}{\lambda^{2}-1}{/tex}
Substituting {tex}\alpha, \beta{/tex} in (i), we get
{tex}\lambda=\frac{\lambda+2}{\lambda^{2}-1}+\frac{4 \lambda+2}{\lambda^{2}-1}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \lambda^{3}-6 \lambda-4=0{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow(\lambda+2)\left(\lambda^{2}-2 \lambda-2\right)=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \lambda{/tex} = -2 (one of the value of {tex}\lambda{/tex})

536045.explanation:
Let f(x) = max. (x + |x|, x - [x]), -2 {tex}\leq{/tex} x < 2
= max. (x - x, x + 2), -2 {tex}\leq{/tex} x < -1
= max. (x - x, x + 1), -1 {tex}\leq{/tex} x < 0
= max. (x + x, x), 0 {tex}\leq{/tex} x < 1
= max. (x + x, x - 1), 1 {tex}\leq{/tex} x < 2

{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} f(x) = x + 2, -2 {tex}\leq{/tex} x < -1
= x + 1, -1 {tex}\leq{/tex} x < 0
= 2x, 0 {tex}\leq{/tex} x < 2
Required integral = area of the shaded region
= {tex}\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{2}{/tex}(2)(4) = 5

536047.explanation:
Let f(x) = min. (|x|, |x - 1|, |x + 1|), -1 {tex}\leq{/tex} x < 1
= min. (-x, 1 - x, x + 1), -1 {tex}\leq{/tex} x < 0
= min. (x, 1 - x, x + 1), 0 {tex}\leq{/tex} x < 1

{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} f(x) = x + 1, -1 {tex}\leq{/tex} < {tex}- \frac {1}{2}{/tex}
= -x, {tex}- \frac {1}{2}{/tex} < x < 0
= x, 0 {tex}\leq{/tex} x < {tex} \frac {1}{2}{/tex}
= 1 - x, {tex}- \frac {1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\leq{/tex} x < 1
Required integral = area of the shaded region
= 2{tex}\left(\frac{1}{2} \cdot 1 \cdot \frac{1}{2}\right){/tex} = {tex} \frac {1}{2}{/tex}

536049.explanation:
Let xsin x = u
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log u = sin x log x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac {1}{u}{/tex}du = (cos x log x + {tex}\frac {1}{x}{/tex}sin x)dx
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} du = xsin x (cos x log x + {tex}\frac {sin x}{x}{/tex})dx
I = {tex}\int x^{\sin x}\left(x\left(\cos x \log x+\frac{\sin x}{x}\right)+1\right){/tex}dx
= {tex}\int x^{\sin x}{/tex}dx + {tex}\int x \cdot x^{\sin x}\left(\cos x \log x+\frac{\sin x}{x}\right){/tex}dx
= {tex}\int(u d x+x d u){/tex} = {tex}\int d(x u){/tex}
= xsin x+1
Required integral = {tex}\left[x^{\sin x+1}\right]_{\frac{\pi}{2}}^{\pi}=\pi-\frac{\pi^{2}}{4}{/tex}

536051.explanation:
Let I = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{2 \pi} \frac{x \sin ^{2 n} x}{\sin ^{2 n} x+\cos ^{2 n} x}{/tex}dx ...(i)
Replacing x by 2{tex}\pi{/tex} - x, we get
I = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{2 \pi} \frac{(2 \pi-x) \sin ^{2 n}(2 \pi-x)}{\sin ^{2 n}(2 \pi-x)+\cos ^{2 n}(2 \pi-x)}{/tex}dx
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{2 \pi} \frac{(2 \pi-x) \sin ^{2 n} x}{\sin ^{2 n} x+\cos ^{2 n} x}{/tex}dx ...(ii)
Adding (i) and (ii), we get
2I = {tex}2 \pi \int \limits_{0}^{2 \pi} \frac{\sin ^{2 n} x}{\sin ^{2 n} x+\cos ^{2 n} x}{/tex}dx
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\pi \int \limits_{0}^{2 \pi} \frac{\sin ^{2 n} x}{\sin ^{2 n} x+\cos ^{2 n} x}{/tex}dx
= {tex}2 \pi \int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\sin ^{2 n} x}{\sin ^{2 n} x+\cos ^{2 n} x}{/tex}dx ...[{tex}\int_{0}^{2 a} f(x){/tex}dx = 2{tex}\int_{0}^{2 a} f(x){/tex}dx, if f(2a - x) = f(x)]
I = {tex}4 \pi \int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin ^{2 n} x}{\sin ^{2 n} x+\cos ^{2 n} x}{/tex}dx ...(iii) ...[{tex}\int_{0}^{2 a} f(x){/tex}dx = 2{tex}\int_{0}^{ a} f(x){/tex}dx, if f(2a - x) = f(x)]
Replacing x by {tex}\frac {\pi}{2}{/tex} - x, we get
I = {tex}4 \pi \int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\cos ^{2 n} x}{\cos ^{2 n} x+\sin ^{2 n} x}{/tex}dx ...(iv)
Adding (iii) and (iv), we get
2I = {tex}4 \pi \int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}4 \pi \times \frac{\pi}{4}=\pi^{2}{/tex}

536053.explanation:
f(x) = x2 + {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{x} e^{-t} f(|x-t|){/tex}dt ...(i)
Replacing t by (x - t), we get
f(x) = x2 + {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{x} e^{t-x}{/tex}f(t)dt
f(x) = x2 + e-x {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{x} e^{t}{/tex} f(t)dt ...(ii)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f'(x) = 2x - e-x{tex}\int \limits_{0}^{x} e^{t}{/tex} f(t) dt + e-x{tex}\cdot{/tex}ex f(x)
= 2x + f(x) - e-x{tex}\int \limits_{0}^{x} e^{t}{/tex} f(t)dt
= 2x + f(x) - (f(x) - x2) ...[From (ii)]
= 2x + x2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f(x) = x2 + {tex}\frac {x^3}{3}{/tex} + c
f(0) = 0 ...[From (i)]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} c = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f(x) = x2 + {tex}\frac {x^3}{3}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f(1) + {tex}\frac {1}{3}{/tex} = {tex}\frac {4}{3}{/tex}

536055.explanation:
In = {tex}\int \limits_{-\pi}^{\pi} \frac{\sin n x}{\left(1+\pi^{x}\right) \sin x}{/tex}dx
= {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\pi}\left(\frac{\sin n x}{\left(1+\pi^{x}\right) \sin x}+\frac{\sin n x}{\left(1+\pi^{-x}\right) \sin x}\right){/tex}dx ...{tex}\left[\int \limits_{-a}^{a} f(x) d x\right.{/tex} = {tex}\left.=\int \limits_{0}^{a}[f(x)+f(-x)] d x\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\sin n x}{\sin x}\left(\frac{1}{1+\pi^{x}}+\frac{n^{x}}{1+\pi^{x}}\right){/tex}dx
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} In = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\sin n x}{\sin x}{/tex}dx
ln+2 - In = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\sin (n+2) x-\sin n x}{\sin x}{/tex}dx
= 2{tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \cos{/tex}(n + 1)x dx = 2{tex}\left[\frac{\sin (n+1) x}{n+1}\right]_{0}^{\pi}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} In+2 - In = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} In+2 = In
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} In = In-2 = In-4 = ... = I4 = I2, if n is even
and In = In-2 = In-4 = ... = I3 = I1, if n is odd
Since, I1 = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\sin x}{\sin x} d x=\pi{/tex}
and I2 = {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\sin 2 x}{\sin x}{/tex}dx
= 2{tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \ cosx{/tex} dx = 2{tex}[\sin x]_{0}^{\pi}{/tex} = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} In = 0, if n is even
= {tex}\pi{/tex}, if n is odd
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\sum \limits_{m=1}^{10} \mathrm{I}_{2 m}{/tex} = 0 and {tex}\sum \limits_{m=1}^{10} \mathrm{I}_{2 m+1}{/tex} = 10{tex}\pi{/tex}

536057.explanation:
{tex}\int \limits_{0}^{2} \frac{\log \left(x^{2}+2\right)}{(x+2)^{2}} {/tex}dx
= {tex}-\left[\frac{\log \left(x^{2}+2\right)}{x+2}\right]_{0}^{2}+\int \limits_{0}^{2} \frac{2 x}{\left(x^{2}+2\right)(x+2)}{/tex}dx
= {tex}-\frac {1}{4}{/tex}log 6 + {tex}\frac {1}{2}{/tex}log 2 + {tex}\int_{0}^{2} \underbrace{\left.\frac{-2}{3(x+2)}+\frac{\frac{2}{3} x+\frac{2}{3}}{x^{2}+2}\right\}}_{\text {by partial fractions }} d x{/tex}
= {tex}-\frac {1}{4}{/tex}log3 - {tex}\frac {1}{4}{/tex}log 2 + {tex}\frac {1}{4}{/tex}log 2 + [-{tex}\frac {2}{3}{/tex}log(x + 2) + {tex}\frac {1}{3}{/tex}log(x2 + 2) + {tex}\left.\frac{\sqrt{2}}{3} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{\sqrt{2}}\right)\right]_{0}^{2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac {1}{4}{/tex}log 2 - {tex}\frac {1}{4}{/tex}log 3 + {tex}\left(-\frac{2}{3} \log 2+\frac{1}{3} \log 3+\frac{\sqrt{2}}{3} \tan ^{-1} \sqrt{2}\right){/tex}
= {tex}\frac {\sqrt 2}{3}{/tex}tan-1{tex}\sqrt 2{/tex} - {tex}\frac {5}{12}{/tex}log2 + {tex}\frac {1}{12}{/tex}log3

536058.explanation:
Let {tex}\vec{d}=x \hat{i}+y \hat{j}+z \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\vec{d} {/tex} is perpendicular to both {tex}\vec a{/tex} and {tex}\vec b{/tex}.
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \vec{d} \cdot \bar{a}=0{/tex} and {tex}\vec{d}+\bar{b}=0{/tex}
Now, {tex}\vec{d} \cdot \vec{a}=0{/tex} {tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 3x + 4y - 2z = 0 ...(i)
{tex}\vec{d} \cdot \vec{b}=0{/tex} {tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 2x + y - z = 0 ...(ii)
{tex}\vec{d} \cdot \vec{c}=8{/tex} {tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 4x - 5y - z = 0 ...(iii)
From (i), (ii) and (iii), we get
x = 2, y = 1 and z = 5
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{d}=2 \hat{i}+\hat{j}+5 \hat{k}{/tex}

536062.explanation:
{tex}\vec{\mathrm{AB}}=-4 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+(p+1) \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\vec{\mathrm{AC}}=2 \hat{i}+(q-1) \hat{j}-3 \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\vec{\mathrm{AB}} \perp \vec{\mathrm{AC}}{/tex} ...[right angled at A]
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \vec{\mathrm{AB}} \cdot \vec{\mathrm{AC}}=0{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} -8 + 2(q - 1) - 3(p + 1) = 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 3p - 2q + 13 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (p, q) lies on the line 3x - 2y + 13 = 0
Slope of line {tex}=\frac{3}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} The point (p, q) lies on a line making an acute angle with the positive direction of x-axis.

536064.explanation:
{tex}\vec{\mathrm{DB}}=\vec{a}-\vec{b}{/tex}
{tex}|\vec{\mathrm{AB}}+\vec{\mathrm{AD}}|=|\vec{\mathrm{AC}}|{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow|\vec{\mathrm{AB}}|^{2}+|\vec{\mathrm{AD}}|^{2}+2 \vec{\mathrm{AB}} \cdot \vec{\mathrm{AD}}{/tex} {tex}=|\vec{\mathrm{AC}}|^{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow|\vec{a}|^{2}+|\bar{b}|^{2}+2 \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=|\vec{c}|^{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=\frac{|\vec{c}|^{2}-|\vec{a}|^{2}-|\vec{b}|^{2}}{2}{/tex} ...(i)
{tex}\vec{\mathrm{DB}} \cdot \vec{\mathrm{AB}}=(\vec{a}-\vec{b}) \cdot \vec{a}{/tex}
{tex}=|\vec{a}|^{2}-\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex}
{tex}=|\vec{a}|^{2}-\frac{|\vec{c}|^{2}-|\vec{a}|^{2}-|\vec{b}|^{2}}{2}{/tex} ...[From (i)]
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{\mathrm{DB}} \cdot \vec{\mathrm{AB}}=\frac{3|\vec{a}|^{2}+|\vec{b}|^{2}-|\vec{c}|^{2}}{2}{/tex}

536067.explanation:
Let {tex}\theta{/tex} be the angle between given vectors.
Let {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} be the given vectors. Then
{tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=|\vec{a}||\vec{b}| \cos \theta{/tex}
{tex}\theta{/tex} is obtuse {tex}\Rightarrow \cos \theta<0 \Leftrightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \bar{b}<0{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} cx2 + 6cx - 12 < 0 for all x.
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} c < 0 and discriminant < 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 36c2 + 48c < 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} c(3c + 4) < 0
{tex}\Rightarrow-\frac{4}{3}{/tex} < c < 0

536069.explanation:
Let {tex}\theta{/tex} be the angle between {tex}\vec a{/tex} and {tex}\vec b{/tex}.
Given, {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\sqrt{3} \vec{c}=\vec{0}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a}+\vec{b}=-\sqrt{3} \vec{c}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow|\vec{a}+\vec{b}|=\sqrt{3}|\vec{c}|{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow|\vec{a}+\vec{b}|^{2}=3|\vec{c}|^{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow|\vec{a}|^{2}+|\vec{b}|^{2}+2|\vec{a}||\vec{b}| \cos \theta=3|\vec{c}|^{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 + 1 + 2 cos {tex}\theta{/tex} = 3
{tex}\Rightarrow \cos \theta=\frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow \theta=\frac{\pi}{3}{/tex}

536071.explanation:
Let {tex}\vec{r}=x \hat{i}+y \hat{j}+z \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\vec{r} \cdot \hat{i}=x, \vec{r} \cdot \hat j=y{/tex} and {tex}\vec{r} \cdot \hat{k}=z{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow(\vec{r} \cdot \hat{i})^{2}+(\vec{r} \cdot \hat{j})^{2}+(\vec{r} \cdot \hat{k})^{2}{/tex} = x2 + y2 + z2
{tex}=|\vec r|^2{/tex}

536073.explanation:
{tex}|\vec{a}-\vec{b}|^{2}+|\vec{b}-\vec{c}|^{2}+|\vec{c}-\vec{a}|^{2}{/tex} {tex}=2\left(|\vec{a}|^{2}+|\vec{b}|^{2}+|\vec{c}|^{2}\right)-2(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}+\vec{c} \cdot \vec{a}){/tex} ...(i)
Also, {tex}|\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}|^{2}{/tex}  {tex}=|\vec{a}|^{2}+|\vec{b}|^{2}+|\vec{c}|^{2}+2(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}+\vec{c} \cdot \vec{a}){/tex} ...(ii)
Adding (i) and (ii), we get
{tex}|\vec{a}-\vec{b}|^{2}+|\vec{b}-\vec{c}|^{2}+|\vec{c}-\vec{a}|^{2}+|\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}|^{2}{/tex} {tex}=3\left(|\vec{a}|^{2}+|\vec{b}|^{2}+|\vec{c}|^{2}\right){/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow 9+|\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}|^{2}=3(3){/tex} ...{tex}[|\vec{a}|=|\vec{b}|=|\vec{c}|=1]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow|\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}|^{2}=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}=\vec{0}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \vec{a}+\vec{b}=-\vec{c}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow|3(\vec{a}+\vec{b})+5 \vec{c}|{/tex} {tex}=|3(-\vec{c})+5 \vec{c}|=|2 \vec{c}|{/tex} = 2

536075.explanation:
{tex}\vec{a} \perp(\vec{b}+\vec{c}){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot(\vec{b}+\vec{c})=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}=0{/tex} ...(i)
Similarly {tex}\vec{b} \cdot \vec{a}+\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}=0{/tex} ...(ii)
{tex}\vec{c} \cdot \vec{a}+\vec{c} \cdot \vec{b}=0{/tex} ...(iii)
Adding (i), (ii) and (iii), we get
{tex}2(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}+\vec{c} \cdot \vec{a})=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+\vec{b} \cdot \bar{c}+\vec{c} \cdot \vec{a}=0{/tex}
Now,
{tex}|\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}|^{2}{/tex} {tex}=|\vec{a}|^{2}+|\vec{b}|^{2}+|\vec{c}|^{2}+2(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+\vec{b} \cdot \bar{c}+\vec{c} \cdot \vec{a}){/tex}
= 9 + 16 + 25 + 0
= 50
{tex}\Rightarrow|\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\bar{c}|=\sqrt{50}=5 \sqrt{2}{/tex}

536077.explanation:
Given, {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}=\vec{0}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow|\vec{a}+\vec{c}|^{2}=|-b|^{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow|\vec{a}|^{2}+|\vec{c}|^{2}+2 \vec{a} \cdot \vec{c} \cos \theta=|\vec{b}|^{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} a2 + c2 + 2ac cos {tex}\theta{/tex} = b2
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \cos \theta=\frac{b^{2}-a^{2}-c^{2}}{2 a c}{/tex}

536079.explanation:
Let the angle between {tex}\vec{a}, \vec{b}{/tex} be {tex}\theta{/tex}. For {tex}\theta{/tex} to be acute {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} > 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \lambda(1-\lambda)+(-1) \lambda+2 \lambda(3)>0{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \lambda-\lambda^{2}-\lambda+6 \lambda>0 \Leftrightarrow \lambda^{2}-6 \lambda<0{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \lambda(\lambda-6)<0{/tex} {tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 0 < {tex}\lambda{/tex} < 6
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} {tex}\lambda{/tex} {tex}\in{/tex} (0, 6)

536082.explanation:
Any vector in the plane of {tex}\vec a{/tex} and {tex}\vec b{/tex} is given by,
{tex}\vec{v}=\lambda \vec{a}+\mu \vec{b}{/tex}, where {tex}\lambda, \mu \in{R}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{v}=\lambda(2 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+\hat{k})+\mu(\hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}){/tex}...(i)
Projection of {tex}\vec v{/tex} on {tex}\vec{c}=\frac{\vec{v} \cdot \vec{c}}{|\vec{c}|}=\frac{1}{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{[(2 \lambda+\mu) \hat{i}+(2 \lambda-2 \mu) \hat{j}+(\lambda+2 \mu) \hat{k}] \cdot(2 \hat{i}+\hat{j}-2 \hat{k})}{\sqrt{2^{2}+1^{2}+2^{2}}}{/tex}= {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \frac{2(2 \lambda+\mu)+1(2 \lambda-2 \mu)-2(\lambda+2 \mu)}{3}=\frac{1}{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow 4 \lambda+2 \mu+2 \lambda-2 \mu-2 \lambda-4 \mu=1{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow 4 \lambda-4 \mu=1 \Leftrightarrow \lambda=\mu+\frac{1}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{v}=\left(\mu+\frac{1}{4}\right)(2 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+\hat{k})+\mu(\hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}){/tex} ...[From (i)]
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{v}=\left(3 \mu+\frac{1}{2}\right) \hat{i}+\left(\frac{1}{2}\right) \hat{j}+\left(3 \mu+\frac{1}{4}\right) \hat{k}{/tex}
Taking {tex}\mu{/tex}= 0
{tex}\vec{v}=\frac{1}{4}(2 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+\hat{k}){/tex}

536086.explanation:
Let {tex}\vec{R}{/tex} be the resultant of three forces and {tex}\vec S {/tex} be the displacement.
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{R}=7 \frac{(\hat{i}+\hat{j})}{\sqrt{1^{2}+1^{2}}}+5 \frac{(\hat{j}+\hat{k})}{\sqrt{1^{2}+1^{2}}}+4 \frac{(\hat{i}+\hat{k})}{\sqrt{1^{2}+1^{2}}}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \vec{\mathrm{R}}=\frac{11 \hat{i}+12 \hat{j}+9 \hat{k}}{\sqrt{2}}{/tex}
and {tex}\vec{d}=(-2 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+\hat{k})-(2 \hat{j}-\hat{k})=-2 \hat{i}+2 \hat{k}{/tex}
Total work done {tex}=\vec{\mathrm{R}} \cdot \vec{d}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{(11 \hat{i}+12 \hat{j}+9 \hat{k})}{\sqrt{2}} \cdot(-2 \hat{i}+2 \hat{k}){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{-22+18}{\sqrt{2}}=\frac{-4}{\sqrt{2}}=-2 \sqrt{2}{/tex}

536088.explanation:
{tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\left|\begin{array}{ccc} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ 2 & -6 & -3 \\ 4 & 3 & -1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} {tex}=5(3 \vec{i}-2 \hat{j}+6 \hat{k}){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow|\bar{a} \times \vec{b}|=5 \sqrt{9+4+36}=35{/tex}
Unit vector perpendicular to {tex}\vec a{/tex} and {tex}\vec b{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\vec{a} \times \vec{b}}{|\vec{a} \times \vec{b}|}=\frac{5}{35}(3 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+6 \hat{k}){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{7}(3 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+6 \hat{k}){/tex}

536092.explanation:

{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\left|\begin{array}{ccc} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ 3 & -4 & 2 \\ 1 & -2 & 3 \end{array}\right|{/tex} {tex}=-8 \hat{i}-7 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex}

Required area {tex}=\frac{1}{2}|\vec{a} \times \vec{b}|=\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{117}{/tex} sq. units

536094.explanation:
{tex}\frac{|\vec{b} \times \vec{c}+\vec{c} \times \vec{a}+\vec{a} \times \vec{b}|}{\vec{\mathrm{BA}} \cdot \vec{\mathrm{BC}}}{/tex} {tex}+\frac{|\vec{c} \times \vec{d}+\vec{d} \times \vec{a}+\vec{a} \times \vec{c}|}{\vec{\mathrm{DA}} \cdot \vec{\mathrm{DC}}}=0{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \frac{2 \operatorname{area} \Delta \mathrm{ABC}}{\vec{\mathrm{BA}}|| \vec{\mathrm{BC}} \mid \cos \mathrm{B}}{/tex} {tex}+\frac{2 \text { area } \Delta \mathrm{ADC}}{|\vec{\mathrm{DA}}||\vec{\mathrm{DC}}| \cos \mathrm{D}}=0{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \frac{2 \text { area } \Delta \mathrm{ABC}}{|\vec{\mathrm{BA}}||\vec{\mathrm{BC}}|} \cdot \frac{\tan \mathrm{B}}{\sin \mathrm{B}}{/tex} {tex}+\frac{2 \text { area } \Delta \mathrm{ADC}}{|\vec{\mathrm{DA}}||\vec{\mathrm{DC}}|} \cdot \frac{\tan \mathrm{D}}{\sin \mathrm{D}}=0{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \frac{2 \text { area } \Delta \mathrm{ABC}}{2 \text { area } \Delta \mathrm{ABC}} \cdot \tan \mathrm{B}{/tex} {tex}+\frac{2 \text { area } \Delta \mathrm{ADC}}{2 \text { area } \Delta \mathrm{ADC}} \cdot \tan \mathrm{D}=0{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} tan B + tan D = 0
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} sin(B + D) = 0 {tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} B + D = {tex}\pi{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} {tex}\square{/tex}ABCD is cyclic

536096.explanation:

{tex}=\left|\begin{array}{ccc} \vec{a} & \vec{b} & \vec{c} \\ 3 & 3 & 1 \\ 3 & 5 & 5 \end{array}\right|=10 \vec{a}-12 \vec{b}+6 \vec{c}{/tex}
{tex}=-2 \hat{i}-20 \hat{j}+22 \hat{k}{/tex}

= 1(4) - 1(2) + 1(-1)
= 1

536100.explanation:
Let {tex}\vec{v}=x \hat{i}+y \hat{j}+z \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\vec{v}, \hat{j}, \hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} are coplanar and
{tex}\vec{v}, \hat{j}-\hat{k}, \hat{i}+\hat{j}{/tex} are also coplanar.
{tex}\Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{lll} \vec{v} & \hat{j} & \hat{j}+\hat{k} \end{array}\right]=0{/tex} and {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} \vec{v} & \hat{j}-\hat{k} & \hat{i}+\hat{j} \end{array}\right]=0{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow\left|\begin{array}{lll} x & y & z \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right|=0{/tex} and {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} x & y & z \\ 0 & 1 & -1 \\ 1 & 1 & 0 \end{array}\right|=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x(1 - 0) = 0 and x(0 + 1) + 1(-y - z) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 0 ...(i)
and x - y - z = 0 ... (ii)
From (i) and (ii), we get
y = - z
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{v}=y \hat{j}-y \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \hat{v}=\frac{y \hat{j}-y \hat{k}}{\sqrt{y^{2}+y^{2}}}=\frac{\hat{j}-\hat{k}}{\sqrt{2}}{/tex}
Let the angle between {tex}\hat v{/tex} (or {tex}\vec v{/tex}) and {tex}(2 \hat{i}+\hat{j}-2 \hat{k}){/tex} be {tex}\theta{/tex}.
{tex}\Rightarrow \cos \theta=\frac{(\hat{j}-\hat{k}) \cdot(2 \hat{i}+\hat{j}-2 \hat{k})}{\sqrt{2} \cdot \sqrt{4+1+4}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{3}{3 \sqrt{2}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \theta{/tex} = 45°

536102.explanation:
{tex}|\vec{a}+\bar{b}|=\sqrt{3}{/tex}
Squaring on both sides, we get
{tex}|\vec{a}|^{2}+|\vec{b}|^{2}+2 \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=3{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 + 1 + 2 cos {tex}\theta{/tex} = 3
{tex}\Rightarrow \cos \theta=\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \theta{/tex} = 60°
{tex}\vec{c}=\vec{a}+2 \vec{b}+3(\vec{a} \times \bar{b}){/tex}
Squaring on both sides, we get
{tex}|\vec{c}|^{2}=|\vec{a}|^{2}+4|\vec{b}|^{2}+9(\vec{a} \times \vec{b})^{2}+4(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}){/tex} {tex}+6 \vec{a} \cdot(\vec{a} \times \vec{b})+12 \vec{b} \cdot(\bar{a} \times \vec{b}){/tex}
= 1 + 4 + 9 sin260° + 4 cos 60° + 0 + 0
{tex}=5+\left(9 \times \frac{3}{4}\right)+\left(4 \times \frac{1}{2}\right){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{55}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow|\vec{c}|=\frac{\sqrt{55}}{2} \Rightarrow 2|\vec{c}|=\sqrt{55}{/tex}

= 0

536108.explanation:
Given that {tex}\vec{a}=k(\vec{b} \times \vec{c}){/tex} ...(i)
{tex}|\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}|^{2}=(\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}) \cdot(\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}){/tex}

536110.explanation:
{tex}\left[\lambda(\vec{a}+\vec{b}) \quad \lambda^{2} \vec{b} \quad \lambda \bar{c}\right]{/tex} {tex}=[\vec{a} \quad \vec{b}+\vec{c} \quad \vec{b}]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \lambda^{4}[\vec{a}+\vec{b} \quad \vec{b} \quad \vec{c}]{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{lll} \vec{a} & \vec{b}+\vec{c} & \vec{b} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \lambda^{4}\left\{\left[\begin{array}{lll} \vec{a} & \vec{b} & \vec{c} \end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{lll} \vec{b} & \vec{b} & \vec{c} \end{array}\right]\right\}{/tex} {tex}=\left\{\left[\begin{array}{lll} \vec{a} & \vec{b} & \vec{b} \end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{lll} \vec{a} & \vec{c} & \vec{b} \end{array}\right]\right\}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \lambda^{4}\left[\begin{array}{lll} \vec{a} & \vec{b} & \vec{c} \end{array}\right]=-\left[\begin{array}{lll} \vec{a} & \vec{b} & \vec{c} \end{array}\right]{/tex}

This is not true for any real value of {tex}\lambda{/tex} as {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} \vec{a} & \vec{b} & \vec{c} \end{array}\right] \neq 0{/tex}.

536113.explanation:

{tex} |\vec{a}|=\sqrt{(1)^2+(-1)^2+(-\sqrt{2})^2}=\sqrt{1+1+2}=\sqrt{4}=2{/tex}
Also, {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=2(1)+2 \sqrt{2}(-1)-2(-\sqrt{2})=2-2 \sqrt{2}+2 \sqrt{2}=2{/tex}
Therefore, the projection of vector {tex}\hat{i}-\hat{j}-\sqrt{2} \hat{k}{/tex} on the vector {tex}\vec{b}=2 \hat{i}+2 \sqrt{2} \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex} is 
{tex} \frac{1}{|\vec{a}|}(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b})=\frac{1}{2}(2)=1{/tex}

536117.explanation:
Given, {tex}(\vec{a} \times \vec{b}) \times(\vec{c} \times \vec{a})=7(\vec{c}+\vec{d}){/tex} ...(i)
Let {tex}\vec{r}=\vec{a} \times \vec{b}{/tex}. Then,
(i) {tex}\Rightarrow \vec{r} \times(\vec{c} \times \vec{d})=7(\vec{c}+\vec{d}){/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow(\vec{r} \cdot \vec{d}) \vec{c}-(\vec{r} \cdot \vec{c}) \vec{d}=7 \vec{c}+7 \vec{d}{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow \vec{r} \cdot \vec{d}=7{/tex} and {tex}\vec{r} \cdot \vec{c}=-7{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow(\vec{a} \times \vec{b}) \cdot \vec{d}=7{/tex} and {tex}(\vec{a} \times \vec{b}) \cdot \vec{c}=-7{/tex} ...(ii)

536127.explanation:

Given, {tex}\vec {b_{2}}=\vec{b}-\vec{b}_{1}{/tex}
{tex}=(2 \hat{i}+\hat{j}-3 \hat{k})-\lambda(3 \hat{i}-\hat{j}){/tex}

{tex}\Leftrightarrow \lambda=\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{b}_{1}=\frac{3}{2} \hat{i}-\frac{1}{2} \hat{j}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{b_{2}}=\frac{1}{2} \hat{i}+\frac{3}{2} \hat{j}-3 \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\vec{b}_{1} \times \overrightarrow{b_{2}}=\left|\begin{array}{ccc} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ \frac{3}{2} & \frac{-1}{2} & 0 \\ \frac{1}{2} & \frac{3}{2} & -3 \end{array}\right|{/tex}
{tex}=\hat{k}\left(\frac{3}{2} \times \frac{3}{2}+\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{1}{2}\right)-3\left(-\frac{1}{2} \hat{i}-\frac{3}{2} \hat{j}\right){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{5}{2} \hat{k}+\frac{3}{2} \hat{i}+\frac{9}{2} \hat{j}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\left|\vec{b}_{1} \times \overrightarrow{b_{2}}\right|=\sqrt{\left(\frac{5}{2}\right)^{2}+\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)^{2}+\left(\frac{9}{2}\right)^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{\frac{25+9+81}{4}}=\frac{\sqrt{115}}{2}{/tex}

536200.explanation:

Let I = {tex}\int \limits_{3}^{7} \sqrt{(x-3)(7-x)} d x{/tex}
= {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{1} \sqrt{4 t \cdot 4(1-t)} \cdot 4 d t{/tex}
= {tex}16 \int \limits_{0}^{1} \sqrt{t(1-t)} d t{/tex}
Let t = sin2u {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dt = 2 sin u cos u du
I = 16{tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \sin u{/tex} cos u {tex}\cdot{/tex} 2sin u cos u du
= {tex}8 \int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \sin ^{2} 2 u{/tex} du
= {tex}4 \int \limits_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(1-\cos 4 u){/tex}du
= {tex}4\left[u-\frac{\sin 4 u}{4}\right]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}4\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)=2 \pi{/tex}
